summary: Excellent sound‑board recording of a legendary September ’72 show – Dark Star, Bird Song and Cumberland Blues sparkle while the band’s playing is top‑notch.
review: The SBD capture is clear and well balanced, delivering the iconic Dark Star → Cumberland transition, a soaring Bird Song and an all‑time great Brokedown Palace.  The first set opens powerfully with Morning Dew and stays tight throughout, while the second set rolls on jazzy jams and flawless guitar work from Jerry; only a few minor vocal slips are noted.  Overall the performance is hailed as one of the band’s finest of ’72.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (≈30 5‑star and 4‑star comments) praise both the musical performance and the soundboard quality; only a handful of isolated negative points keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: SBD is described as fine/crisp throughout.  Minor complaints about sloppy vocals and one out‑of‑tune moment, but no major dropouts or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid and energetic, opening with Morning Dew and closing with a tight Playin' in the Band.  Second set features the legendary Dark Star → Cumberland sequence, followed by jazzy jams (China>Rider, Bird Song) that keep the momentum high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Blistering guitar work especially on Dark Star; praised for soaring solos and overall delivery, though a reviewer noted a sloppy vocal moment and a mis‑step in the Casey Jones solo.
band_member_comments.Phil: Recognized for jazzy rhythmic contributions and solid bass foundation throughout the jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s jazz‑savvy playing highlighted on Dark Star, adding depth to the improvisations.
